LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT W. COYLE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST, THIRD, FIFTH, SEVENTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED VERIFIED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD, SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS ADEQUATELY PLED. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE: 1) FRAUD WITH PARTICULARITY; 2) DISGORGEMENT AS A REMEDY BUT NOT AS A CAUSE OF ACTION; AND 3) THE ELEMENTS OF THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE AND THAT DOCTRINE AS A BASIS TO MAKE THIS DEFENDANT LIABLE FOR THE CONDUCT OF HIS ALTER EGO UNDER ONE OR MORE CLAIMS BUT NOT AS AN INDEPENDENT CAUSE OF ACTION. A CLAIM FOR CONVERSION DOES NOT LIE ON THESE FACTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THP-SF, INC.'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B). RELIEF UNDER CCP 473.5 IS INAPPROPRIATE BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHOW THAT IT LACKED ACTUAL NOTICE OF THIS SUIT. ALTHOUGH THERE IS SCANT EVIDENCE SHOWING MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT, CCP 473(B) IS APPLIED LIBERALLY WHERE THE PARTY SEEKING RELIEF MOVES DILIGENTLY AND THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO THE PARTY OPPOSING THE MOTION. (SEE ELSTON V. CITY OF TURLOCK (1985) 38 CAL.3D 227, 233-234.) DEFENDANT HAS TEN DAYS TO FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN P. GREENQUIST & ATTY. G. RYAN PATRICK OF THE WIEGEL LAW GRUP, TEL. NO. 552-8230 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. VANESSA O. BEARY OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WB COYLE AND TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS FROM 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled their alter ego allegations. The third cause of action is not duplicative of the second cause of action and therefore is not improperly included in the Third Amended Complaint. The Prayer for Disgorgement of Commissions is properly pled because Plaintiffs are entitled to seek monetary damages and equitable relief in the alternative. (See Ripling v. Sup.Ct. (Norton) (1952) 112 Cal.App.2d 399, 408.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Aug-07-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN P. GREENQUIST & ATTY. G. RYAN PATRICK OF THE WIEGEL LAW GRUP, TEL. NO. 552-8230 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. VANESSA O. BEARY OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THP-SF, INC.'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT is Granted pursuant to CCP 473(b). Relief under CCP 473.5 is inappropriate because Defendant failed to show that it lacked actual notice of this suit. Although there is scant evidence showing mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect, CCP 473(b) is applied liberally where the party seeking relief moves diligently and there is no prejudice to the party opposing the motion. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233-234.) Defendant has ten days to file a responsive pleading. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG
